Actually, searching for "Pearl of Africa" and "SPFMV" and "SPCSV" often leads to the work of **Adikini et al. (2016)** or similar.

However, the most standard method for these viruses in field surveys in Uganda is **NCM-ELISA** (Nitrocellulose Membrane Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay). The kit used is almost always the one provided by the **International Potato Center (CIP)**.

The two most commonly used chemicals (often reagents/substrates) in the NCM-ELISA detection method for these viruses are **Nitro Blue Tetrazolium (NBT)** and **5-Bromo-4-chloro-3-indolyl phosphate (BCIP)**. These are used as the substrate for the alkaline phosphatase enzyme.
